summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host data and scans for crypto wallets to attacker-controlled endpoint
details: Package ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that collectively perform host-information collection, filesystem scanning, and outbound HTTPS exfiltration. scanner.js contains a hardcoded C2 endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 161, 323) reached via https.request/https.get with POST, with the request payload composed of os.hostname(), username (whoami via execSync), and cwd (lines 234, 246, 316, 694, 1046, 1048). wallet-scanner.js performs filesystem walks combined with child_process exec (curl, ping) and HTTPS POST calls — the canonical crypto-wallet/seed-phrase scanner shape. build-verifier.js similarly reads os/fs and POSTs to a remote endpoint. The use of a github.io static page as a beacon target, combined with wallet enumeration and host fingerprinting, is unambiguous attacker tooling. The package name ("defi-env-auditor") is a cover story; auditing tools do not exfiltrate hostnames+usernames+wallet data to a random GitHub Pages site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
